title: H5N1 confirmed in poultry farm, Baden-Württemberg, Germany — near Swiss border
summary: ECDC reports H5N1 HPAI confirmation in a commercial broiler farm in the Lörrach district of Baden-Württemberg, Germany, approximately 15 km from the Swiss border at Basel. 18,000 birds affected with rapid mortality. Clade 2.3.4.4b confirmed. German authorities have established restriction zones and notified neighbouring countries including Switzerland.
raw_content: ECDC Rapid Risk Assessment Update: HPAI A(H5N1) — Germany. Commercial broiler farm in Lörrach district, Baden-Württemberg. 18,000 birds, rapid onset mortality. Clade 2.3.4.4b. Distance to Swiss border: approximately 15 km. Restriction zones established. Cross-border notification to CH, FR initiated.
analysis: This is a critical development for Swiss animal health authorities. An H5N1 outbreak in a commercial poultry farm just 15 km from Basel puts Swiss poultry farms in the immediate risk zone. The Lörrach district is directly adjacent to cantons Basel-Stadt and Basel-Landschaft. BLV should immediately alert cantonal veterinary offices in northwestern Switzerland, intensify active surveillance in poultry holdings within 50 km of the border, and verify biosecurity measures are in place. Wild bird surveillance along the Rhine should be prioritized.

title: H5N1 confirmed in wild birds along Rhine, Baden-Württemberg, Germany
summary: WOAH notification reports H5N1 HPAI in wild birds (mallards and grey herons) found dead along the Rhine near Breisach, Baden-Württemberg, Germany. 8 carcasses tested positive. The location is along the Rhine corridor approximately 60 km north of Basel. This detection is epidemiologically linked to the Lörrach poultry outbreak.
raw_content: WOAH Immediate Notification: HPAI A(H5N1) — wild birds, Germany (Baden-Württemberg). 8 dead birds (Anas platyrhynchos, Ardea cinerea) positive near Breisach am Rhein. Same clade 2.3.4.4b as Lörrach poultry outbreak. Rhine corridor surveillance intensified.
analysis: Wild bird detections along the Rhine corridor between Breisach and Lörrach confirm active H5N1 circulation in the immediate vicinity of the Swiss border. The Rhine serves as a major migratory flyway and wintering habitat for waterfowl that move freely between Germany and Switzerland. This significantly elevates the risk of H5N1 introduction into Swiss wild bird populations and subsequently domestic poultry. Enhanced wild bird surveillance along the Swiss Rhine and Lake Constance is urgently recommended.

title: Dengue emergency declared in southern Vietnam — Ho Chi Minh City overwhelmed
summary: Vietnam's Ministry of Health has declared a dengue emergency in southern provinces including Ho Chi Minh City, Dong Nai, and Binh Duong. 42,000 cases and 67 deaths reported in 2026. Hospitals in Ho Chi Minh City report ICU capacity strain. DENV-2 is the dominant serotype. Vector control campaigns intensified.
raw_content: ProMED-mail post: Dengue — Vietnam (southern provinces). Emergency declared. 42,000 cases, 67 deaths in 2026. DENV-2 dominant. Hospital capacity strained in HCMC. Vector control scaled up. WHO providing technical assistance.
analysis: Vietnam's dengue emergency reflects an intensifying outbreak across Southeast Asia. The dominance of DENV-2, combined with a population previously exposed primarily to DENV-1, raises concerns about severe dengue presentations due to antibody-dependent enhancement. ICU capacity strain in Ho Chi Minh City may lead to increased mortality. Swiss travel medicine providers should ensure pre-travel counselling includes dengue prevention for Vietnam travellers.

title: African swine fever in wild boar, eastern Poland — Podlaskie voivodeship
pathogen: African swine fever virus genotype II
summary: WOAH notification reports 6 confirmed ASF cases in wild boar in the Podlaskie voivodeship of eastern Poland, near the Belarus border. ASFV genotype II confirmed. Hunting restrictions and wild boar population management measures reinforced in the affected area.
raw_content: WOAH Immediate Notification: African swine fever — Poland (Podlaskie). 6 wild boar carcasses positive for ASFV genotype II. Location near Belarus border. Ongoing surveillance in established ASF zone. Domestic pig farms in area under enhanced biosecurity.
analysis: ASF continues to circulate in wild boar populations in eastern Poland, consistent with the established endemic zone near the Belarus border. While geographically distant from Switzerland, the westward expansion of ASF in Europe over recent years remains a concern for BLV. The detection does not represent a significant change in the ASF situation in Poland.

title: Legionnaires' disease cluster in tourists returning from Dubai
summary: ECDC reports a cluster of 7 Legionnaires' disease cases in European tourists who visited Dubai between February 15-25, 2026. Cases reported from Germany (3), Netherlands (2), and Switzerland (2). L. pneumophila serogroup 1 confirmed. Investigation of hotel water systems underway.
raw_content: ECDC Rapid Communication: Legionnaires' disease cluster — travel-associated, Dubai. 7 confirmed cases in European tourists. DE (3), NL (2), CH (2). All stayed at hotels in Dubai Marina area. Feb 15-25 exposure period. Investigation ongoing.
analysis: Two Swiss cases in this travel-associated Legionnaires' disease cluster make this directly relevant for BAG. The hotel water system investigation in Dubai is critical to prevent further cases. Swiss public health authorities should ensure the two cases are followed up and that travel health alerts are issued for the implicated accommodation. This is a contained environmental exposure rather than a communicable disease threat.
